REQUEST TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ROBERSON, ESSENCE ORDER FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS GRANTED PURSUANT TO G.C. 68634 (E), CRC 3.52
SERVICE OF COMPLAINT BY CERTIFIED MAIL FILED BY PLAINTIFF ROBERSON, ESSENCE AS TO DEFENDANT METHENEY, JOHN
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF SERVICE FEE WAS $50.00 (WAIVED) SERVED MAY-28-2025, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT METHENEY, JOHN
DECLARATION OF DEFENDANT STATING HIS CORRECT NAME FILED BY DEFENDANT METHENEY, JOHN
CLAIM OF DEFENDANT FOR $12,490.00 FILED BY METHENEY, JOHN AS TO CROSS DEFENDANT ROBERSON, ESSENCE ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR JUL-16-2025 AT 01:30 PM IN DEPT 506
DECLARATION OF DUE DILIGENCE IN ATTEMPTING SERVICE SERVICE FEE WAS $50.00 FILED BY DEFENDANT METHENEY, JOHN AS TO PLAINTIFF ROBERSON, ESSENCE
REQUEST TO POSTPONE SMALL CLAIMS HEARING FILED BY DEFENDANT METHENEY, JOHN
ORDER ON REQUEST TO POSTPONE TRIAL DENIED*** PER REQUEST OF DEFENDANT.

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF JUL-16-2025 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF JUL-16-2025 BY VISITING JUDGE TAMILA IPEMA. APPEARANCES: PLAINTIFF ESSENCE ROBERSON IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT JOHN METHENEY IS PRESENT. CLERK: M. DE LUNA. NO EXHIBITS RETAINED. NOT REPORTED. (D506)

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF JUL-16-2025 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF JUL-16-2025 BY VISITING JUDGE TAMILA EPEMA. APPEARANCES: CROSS-COMPLAINANT JOHN METHENEY IS PRESENT. CROSS-DEFENDANT ESSENCE ROBERSON IS PRESENT. CLERK: M. DE LUNA. NO EXHIBITS RETAINED. NOT REPORTED (D506)
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUL-16-2025 - JUDGMENT.

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF ROBERSON, ESSENCE IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY DEFENDANT METHENEY, JOHN PARTIES KNEW THEY WERE ENTERING INTO ILLEGAL SUBLEASE AGREEMENTS ON COMMERCIAL PROPERTY. DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE THE AUTHORITY TO SUBLEASE THE PROPERTY TO PLAINTIFF.PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED SHE HELD A PUBLIC EVENT IN THE LOCATION WHILE THERE WERE DEAD RATS UNDER THE TABLES & PUBLIC SEATS. THEY BOTH KNOWINGLY ACTED ILLEGALLY IN AGREEING TO HOLD A PUBLIC EVENT IN AN INFESTED RAT HOUSE WITH DIRTY EQUIPMENT INSTEAD OF CANCELLING THE DEAL & THE EVENT, ENDANGERING THE PUBLIC SAFETY. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS, SEE JUDGMENT ATTACHED NO EXHIBITS FILED
REMARK:RETURNED MAIL FROM THE POST OFFICE. 07/24/2025. SC-152 ORDER ON REQUEST TO POSTPONE TRIAL MAILED TO PLAINTIFF. (RETURN TO SENDER NOSUCH NUMBER UNABLE TO FORWARD RETURN TO SENDER)
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUL-16-2025 - JUDGMENT.

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT CROSS COMPLAINANT METHENEY, JOHN IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY CROSS DEFENDANT ROBERSON, ESSENCE PARTIES KNEW THEY WERE ENTERING INTO ILLEGAL SUBLEASE AGREEMENTS ON COMMERCIAL PROPERTY. DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE THE AUTHORITY TO SUBLEASE THE PROPERTY TO PLAINTIFF.PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED SHE HELD A PUBLIC EVENT IN THE LOCATION WHILE THERE WERE DEAD RATS UNDER THE TABLES & PUBLIC SEATS. THEY BOTH KNOWINGLY ACTED ILLEGALLY IN AGREEING TO HOLD A PUBLIC EVENT IN AN INFESTED RAT HOUSE WITH DIRTY EQUIPMENT INSTEAD OF CANCELLING THE DEAL & THE EVENT, ENDANGERING THE PUBLIC SAFETY. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS, SEE JUDGMENT ATTACHED
